title: Senator Shannon Grove (R-Bakersfield) announced that she has introduced three bills to encourage the ethical use of energy in California:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Bill 974 will ensure local communities benefit from lithium mining in California.",
    "Senate Bill 1087 measures the carbon emissions of transporting foreign oil to California, while also considering the human rights and environmental policies of the exporting countries.",
    "Senate Bill 1239 requires that suppliers of electric vehicles to the State of California must certify that the raw materials in zero-emission vehicles purchased by the state come from child-labor free mining enterprises.",
    "California produces less than 400,000 barrels of oil per day, which is significantly lower than its daily need of 1.8 million barrels.",
    "About 42 percent of California's oil imports come directly from the Amazon Rainforest in Ecuador, Brazil, Guyana, Argentina, and Peru.",
    "Much of the crude oil imported into California comes from foreign nations with demonstrated human rights abuses or lower environmental standards than California.",
    "SB 1087 will require the Air Resources Board to report the amount of particulate matter released into the air from tanker ships that import oil into California.",
    "California state government agencies are required to increase the number of zero emission vehicles within the state fleet to fifty percent of new purchases by 2024/25.",
    "The cobalt needed for electric car batteries is primarily mined in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, where many workers endure poor environmental and working conditions.",
    "In 2009, the U.S. Department of Labor placed cobalt ore from the Democratic Republic of the Congo on its list of goods produced by child labor or forced labor.",
    "Child labor persists in cobalt production, raising risks for the lithium-ion battery supply chain.",
    "SB 1239 requires suppliers of electric vehicles to certify that the raw materials in zero-emission vehicles come from child-labor free mining enterprises.",
    "SB 974 ensures that all communities impacted by domestic lithium production receive a portion of the lithium extraction excise tax.",
    "Currently, the main source of lithium production in California is Imperial County, specifically near the Salton Sea.",
    "SB 974 ensures a portion of the revenues generated from lithium mining will stay in the communities directly impacted by that mining."
  ]
}
```
